english: ०धर (०dhara), also lág-na (lag na), or pýag-na-rdo-rje (phyag na rdo rje), and abbreviated lag- (lag-), or pýag-rdór (phyag rdor), holder of the sceptre, originally the Indra of the Brahmans; in Buddhism, in the first place, the Dhyani Bodhisatva of the Dhyani Buddha Aksobhya, and secondly a terrifying deity, the guardian of the mystical doctrine (Was. frq.), hence confounded with the čos-skyoṅ-bži (chos skyong bzhi), as well as with ku-be-ra (ku be ra), prince of the gnod-sbyin (gnod sbyin), and special deity of Milaraspa; v. Köpp. and Schl. — rdo-rje-gdán (rdo rje gdan), वज्रासन, prop. the diamond seat or throne of Buddha at Gaya, Köpp. I, 93, and hence also proper name applied to that town, frq. — rdo-rje-pʻá-lám (rdo rje pha lam) diamond v. above. — rdo-rje-pʻág-mo (rdo rje phag mo), वज्रवाराहि or भट्टारि (Wts. 136) 'diamond-sow', a goddess of later Buddhism, frq. worshipped (also in Lh., where she has a sanctuary at Markula near Triloknath), and incarnated as abbess in a nunnery, situated on an island of the lake Pal-te, v. Georgi Alph. Tib., Wts. 135. — rdo-rje-pʻúr-pa (rdo rje phur pa) Glr. an instrument the upper part of which is a dorje and the lower a purpa. — rdo-rje-légs-pa (rdo rje legs pa), abbrev. *dor-lág* (*dor lag*), a local deity in Lh., originally an honest village black-smith. — rdo-rje-séms-dpa (rdo rje sems dpa'), वज्रसत्त्व, gen. = rdo-rje-čáṅ (rdo rje chang) (Was. 188), sometimes differing from it, v. Schl. p. 50; also = mi-skyód-pa (mi bskyod pa), Aksobhya; also mi-skyoṅ-rdo-rje (mi skyong rdo rje) Glr. Respecting the word rdo-rje (rdo rje) cf. Burn. I, 526.
rdo-ra (rdo ra), or rto-ra (rto ra) circle of dancers W.
rdog (rdog) C. root, *dog dhaṅ ló-ma* (*dog dang lo ma*) root and leaves; *lab-dog* (*lab dog*) radish-root; yet cf. rdóg-po (rdog po).

rdóg-pa (rdog pa) step, footstep; kick, rdóg-pa bór-ba (rdog pa 'bor ba) to step, to pace, to walk Cs.; rdóg-sgra (rdog sgra) the sound of steps, the clattering of hoofs; rdog-stáṅ (rdog stang) a straw-mat for cleaning one's shoes C.; rdóg-pai óg-tu ǰúg-pa (rdog pa'i 'og tu 'jug pa) Dzl. 782, 13 (Ms.; Sch.: rdóg-pai żábs-su?) (rdog pa'i zhabs su) to prostrate, to throw under one's feet; rdóg-pas rdúṅ-ba (rdog pas rdung ba) Sch., pʻúl-ba (phul ba) Sch., snón-pa (snon pa), mnán-pa (mnan pa) Sch., rdog-pʻúl rgyáb-pa (rdog phul rgyab pa) Pth., *dog-tó pʻúl-wa* (*dog to phul wa*) C., *dog-čóṅ gyab-če* (*dog chong gyab ce*) W. to strike with the foot, to apply a good kick, to stamp the ground; rdog-bstád byéd-pa (rdog bstad byed pa) prob. id.; prop. to load, to pack on (?).

rdóg-po (rdog po) (Cs. also rdóg-ma (rdog ma)), a grain of corn, sand, sugar; a drop of rain Glr.; sran rdog bdun (sran rdog bdun) seven peas; pʻreṅ-rdog (phreng rdog) the bead of a rosary, which often consists of grains of seed; a piece, rdog-gčíg (rdog gcig) (how many turnips do you want?) one C.
rdor (rdor) 1. in compound words for rdó-rje (rdo rje). — 2. n. of a monastery in Tibet Cs. Chronolog. Table 1223 p. C. — 3. = sdor (sdor) Cs.
rdól-pa (rdol pa) a cobbler Cs., prob. = gdól-pa (gdol pa).

rdól-ba (rdol ba), pf. and fut. brdol (brdol), vb. n. to rtól-ba (rtol ba), 1. to come out, to break forth from, to gush forth, to issue from, of a well of water (issuing from) Pth; to come up, to sprout, to shoot, of seed; *so ma dol* (*so ma dol*) W. the teeth are not yet cutting; kóṅ-nas rdól-bai glu (kong nas rdol ba'i glu) a song streaming forth from within Mil.; mi-nad rdól-žiṅ (mi nad rdol zhing) diseases breaking out among men Mil.; to flow or run off, of the water of a lake; kloṅ rdól-ba (klong rdol ba) to come forth, to proceed from the middle or the midst of Glr. (the meaning of this passage is not quite clear); rdol-gzér (rdol gzer) an instrument for boring metals Sch. — 2. of vessels: to leak, to be not tight, to have holes, snod żabs-brdól (snod zhabs brdol) a vessel with a leaky bottom Thgy.; also of shoes, covers, tent-cloth etc. not being watertight; to break, to burst, of ulcers, wounds; glo-rdol (glo rdol) Med. v. gló-ba (glo ba); rdol-gnyán (rdol gnyan) Sch.: 'fistula; gonorrhea'. — 3. to rave, to delirate; to be sleep-walking, lunatic, also bla rdól(smrá)-ba (bla rdol smra ba) Lex., where it is explained by bab-čól (bab col); gnyid-rdól (gnyid rdol), mig-rdól (mig rdol) C. id.
rdós-pa (rdos pa) 1. sbst., Cs. = gdos (gdos); lus rdos-čé (lus rdos che) Lex. w.e. — 2. vb. n. Sch.: 'to break, burst, flow out, dbú-ba (dbu ba), or lbú-ba (lbu ba) the bursting of a bubble'.
lda... (lda...) Ld. frq. for kla... (kla...), gla... (gla...), zla... (zla...).
